title: GPU-Based HPC Cluster Build
summary: Built and optimized GPU clusters (NVIDIA DGX, GKE nodes, Cisco UCS) for AI workloads.

content.value: TouchNet's AI workloads outgrew shared cloud VMs. Training runs for large models were taking 10+ hours due to resource contention and lack of GPU scheduling. A purpose-built HPC environment was needed.

Designed and provisioned a hybrid GPU cluster combining NVIDIA DGX A100 nodes on-premises, GKE GPU node pools in GCP, and Cisco UCS blades for CPU-bound preprocessing. Ansible playbooks automated OS provisioning, CUDA driver installation, and DCGM exporter setup for GPU telemetry.

Kubernetes GPU resource quotas and node affinity rules ensured fair scheduling across teams. A custom autoscaler scaled GKE GPU nodes up during peak training windows and down overnight, cutting idle GPU costs by 40%.
Outcome: model training throughput increased 3×, idle GPU spend dropped 40%, and the cluster handled 20+ concurrent experiments without contention.
